Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cmi): correct sample playback speed — was 11x too slow ("whale")

Root cause: CMI hardware pitch model reads wave_ram at ~1220 samples/sec
for middle C, designed for 128-sample page loops. Real Fairlight samples
at 14080 Hz would play at 0.087x speed — 13 seconds for a 1-second sample.

Fix: Added sample_mode flag — when a sample >256 bytes is loaded,
processSample() uses linear addressing with sample_inc = playRate/sampleRate
where playRate = 14080 * (freq/261.63). Hardware page-stepping mode preserved
for built-in waveforms.

Verified: 261 Hz output for MIDI 60 with 14080 Hz test sine (ratio 0.998x).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix lock signal scope for cross-GPU visibility in matmul_all_reduce (#477)

Use scope="sys" instead of scope="gpu" for the lock-signal atomic in
_fused_matmul_all_reduce_kernel. The lock array lives on the symmetric
heap mapped via IPC, so atomics consumed by remote ranks must use
system scope for xGMI visibility. Fixes intermittent hangs on
multi-GPU runs.

Fixes #462

Co-Authored-By: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Song Len display for GT Ultra: read from order data not tracker store

GT Ultra has its own order list system separate from the standard
tracker store's patternOrder. The FT2Toolbar was showing songLength=1
(the default tracker store value) instead of the actual GT Ultra order
list length computed from channel 0's order data up to the 0xFF
terminator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix pattern length using table channels (255 rows) instead of pattern channels

formatChannelsToSnapshot used Math.max across ALL channels including
table channels (WAVE/PULSE/FLTR/SPEED at 255 rows), making the pattern
editor appear infinitely long. Now only considers isPatternChannel
channels for the pattern length.

commit.message:
Fix SSE race condition with deterministic done_published_at flag (#1261)

* Initial plan

* fix(app): drain SSE event queue for late-connecting clients on finished jobs

When a client connects to the SSE events endpoint after a job has
already finished, the endpoint would previously yield only 'state'
and 'done' events, skipping any 'log', 'progress', or 'artifact'
events that were queued during the job's execution.

This fix drains the subscriber's event queue before yielding the
synthetic 'done' event, ensuring late-connecting clients receive
all events that were published during the job's lifecycle.

Fixes CI failure on main branch where
test_job_events_stream_emits_state_log_progress_artifact_done
was flaky due to a race condition between job completion and
SSE client connection timing.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/9eae8c67-ab90-41cb-beea-99cbde490ff1

* fix(app): address code review - use atomic queue drain pattern

- Remove race-prone `while not q.empty()` check
- Use `while True` with `QueueEmpty` exception for atomic drain
- Add documentation explaining the two 'done' event paths

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/9eae8c67-ab90-41cb-beea-99cbde490ff1

* fix(workflows): add workflow-level permissions and pin actions to fix CodeQL Actions analysis

Add workflow-level permissions blocks to workflows that only had job-level
permissions. CodeQL's MissingActionsPermissions query requires workflow-level
permissions for security best practices.

Also pin several unpinned action references (checkout@v6, setup-python@v6)
to their commit SHAs to address potential UseOfKnownVulnerableAction findings.

Workflows fixed:
- codeql.yml: Add workflow-level permissions, pin checkout action
- apex_performance.yml: Add workflow-level permissions
- apex_policy_validation.yml: Add workflow-level permissions
- ingest_contract_validation.yml: Add workflow-level permissions, pin actions
- submit-pypi.yml: Add workflow-level permissions, pin actions
- summary.yml: Add workflow-level permissions

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/b5ee0b98-2496-4c38-9ace-1c7aebbc91d5

* fix(tests): update test_workflows_use_modern_actions to accept SHA-pinned actions

The test was failing because it only matched version tags like @v6 but not
commit SHA pins with version comments like @<sha> # v6. Update regex patterns
to accept both formats.

Fixes CI failure in: test_pypi_workflows.py::TestPyPIWorkflows::test_workflows_use_modern_actions

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/52c1afe7-fb3f-4ff1-9930-b0b4e6bb7eed

* fix(tests): handle uppercase hex in commit SHA regex patterns

Git commit SHAs can contain uppercase letters A-F. Update regex patterns
to use [a-fA-F0-9] instead of [a-f0-9] to handle all valid SHA formats.

Address code review feedback.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/52c1afe7-fb3f-4ff1-9930-b0b4e6bb7eed

* style: apply Black formatting to test_pypi_workflows.py

Black reformatted multi-line regex pattern to single line.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/af846206-8c6c-4776-88a6-48e746e50759

* fix: address Copilot review comments on PR #1261

1. tests/test_pypi_workflows.py: Fix misleading comment that said "@v7"
   but actually accepts v4+. Updated to clarify the pattern matches any
   major version and the assertion enforces v4+ minimum.

2. .github/workflows/submit-pypi.yml: Pin all remaining unpinned actions
   to commit SHAs for consistent security posture (CWE-1395):
   - actions/upload-artifact@v7 -> @bbbca2ddaa5d...# v7
   - actions/download-artifact@v8.0.1 -> @70fc10c6e5e1...# v8
   - pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish: add # v1 version comment

3. app.py: Fix SSE event race condition where job.finished_at is set
   before artifact/done events are published. Late-connecting clients
   now wait briefly (0.5s) for real events before generating synthetic
   done, preventing dropped artifact events.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/65f345ee-8200-4aef-98f7-758df7c74608

* fix: apply deterministic fix for SSE late-connect race condition

Addresses code review feedback requesting a deterministic fix instead
of a timed wait:

1. Add `done_published_at` field to Job class to track when terminal
   events have actually been published to subscribers

2. In `_run_job()`, move artifact indexing and event publication to
   BEFORE setting finished_at/done_published_at. This ensures:
   - Late-connecting clients see done_published_at=None during indexing
   - They wait for real events instead of synthesizing prematurely

3. In `job_events()` SSE endpoint:
   - Check done_published_at (not finished_at) for synthesis decision
   - Only synthesize when done_published_at is set (events published)
   - Fall through to event loop when finished_at set but not done_published_at

4. Add regression tests:
   - test_late_connecting_client_receives_real_events_during_artifact_indexing
   - test_late_connecting_client_synthesizes_done_after_done_published_at

5. Fix setup-python regex in test_pypi_workflows.py to use \d+ instead
   of [56] for future-proofing (supports v7+ when released)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/4b071571-efaa-43e9-900f-6a481104f6fd

* fix: set done_published_at in tests to prevent SSE endpoint hangs

Tests that create finished jobs and test SSE endpoints were hanging
because they set finished_at but not done_published_at. The SSE
endpoint now relies on done_published_at to determine when it's safe
to synthesize a done event. Without it, the endpoint falls through
to the event loop and waits forever for events that never come.

Fixed tests:
- test_v1_routes_enforce_api_key_for_reads_and_events
- test_archive_gate_pipeline_submission_returns_job_envelope
- test_job_events_stream_emits_state_log_progress_artifact_done

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/e1ef55b6-f89c-4fd4-b0db-b72e5a3b4b03

* fix: clarify comment about finished_at and done_published_at in regression test

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/e1ef55b6-f89c-4fd4-b0db-b72e5a3b4b03

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra row position: divide pattptr by 4 (byte offset → row index)

pattptr is a byte offset into pattern data (4 bytes/row: note, inst,
cmd, data). gt_get_current_row() was returning the raw byte offset,
causing the pattern editor to jump 4 positions per actual row advance.

Also handle the ENDPATT sentinel (0x7fffffff) by returning 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Smooth pattern editor scrolling for GT Ultra playback

Added FormatPlaybackState singleton — a lightweight format-agnostic
playback state that any format engine can write to. The
PatternEditorCanvas RAF loop reads it every frame (no React props in
the hot path) and computes smooth sub-pixel scroll offset using
time-based interpolation, identical to normal mode.

Changes:
- New: FormatPlaybackState.ts — tracks row, timestamp, rolling-average
  row duration for smooth interpolation
- GTUltra worklet: increase position update rate from 32 blocks (~93ms)
  to 2 blocks (~5.8ms) for responsive row tracking
- useGTUltraEngineInit: pipes position callbacks to FormatPlaybackState
- FT2Toolbar: sets FormatPlaybackState playing on play/stop
- PatternEditorCanvas: format mode RAF loop reads FormatPlaybackState
  directly (not React props) and computes smoothOffset per frame

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate threads to resolve deadlock on stop

Replace threaded AudioCapturer with single-threaded tick pattern:
- Remove capture_audio() and batch_process_buffer() thread entry points
- Add start()/tick()/stop() methods called from main loop
- Use queue.Queue for thread-safe sounddevice callback bridge
- Fix O(n) VAD check: extract only tail 1.5s instead of full buffer concat
- Replace blocking get_key() with non-blocking poll_key() using select
- Remove Thread/Event from AppSession, simplify to delegation

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra pattern editor not following playback

useGTUltraFormatData was reading isPlaying/currentRow from
useTransportStore (the standard tracker engine transport), but GT Ultra
has its own WASM engine with its own playback state. The transport store
values were always false/0 during GT Ultra playback.

Now reads playing and playbackPos.row from useGTUltraStore, which is
correctly updated by the worklet's position callback every 32 frames.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra silent playback: initialize editorInfo in gt_play()

gt_play() didn't initialize editorInfo.maxSIDChannels, adparam, or
multiplier before starting the sequencer. When .sng files lack the
ID=0x1f extended info block, these stay at 0 (the C global default).

With maxSIDChannels=0, the playroutine() channel loop in gplay.c
skips ALL channels — no notes are sequenced, producing a brief thud
(initial SID gate) then silence.

Added the same safety defaults already used by gt_export_prg/sid:
  maxSIDChannels=3, adparam=0x0f00, multiplier=1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(output): fall back to show-option for tmux < 3.3a set-clipboard

#{set-clipboard} format variable is only available since tmux 3.3a (Sep 2022).
On older tmux (Ubuntu 22.04: 3.2a, RHEL 9: 3.2a, Debian 11: 3.1c), it returns
an empty string, causing the previous code to incorrectly fall back to "on"
even when the user had set-clipboard off.

Fix: extract parse_display_message() as a pure function returning Option for the
set-clipboard field. When None (empty response), fall back to
`tmux show-option -gv set-clipboard`, which works on all tmux versions.

Adds 5 unit tests for parse_display_message covering: on, off, external,
empty (< 3.3a), and no-pid cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Merge 1.21.0 back to dev (#1738)

## PR Title Format

**Required Format:** `[Keyword1] [Keyword2]: Description`

- **Keyword1:** `major`, `minor`, or `patch` (case-insensitive)
- **Keyword2:** `feature`, `bugfix`, `engg`, or `tests`
(case-insensitive)

**Examples:**
- `[MAJOR] [Feature]: new API`
- `[minor] [bugfix]: fix crash`
- `[PATCH][tests]:add coverage`

## Proposed changes

Merge 1.21.0 back to dev

## Type of change

- [ ] Feature work
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Engineering change
- [ ] Test
- [ ] Logging/Telemetry

## Risk

- [ ] High – Errors could cause MAJOR regression of many scenarios.
(Example: new large features or high level infrastructure changes)
- [ ] Medium – Errors could cause regression of 1 or more scenarios.
(Example: somewhat complex bug fixes, small new features)
- [ ] Small – No issues are expected. (Example: Very small bug fixes,
string changes, or configuration settings changes)

## Additional information

---------

Fix ScrollToEnd bypassing ShortTranscriptMaxScroll guard in dispatched callbacks

The guard was only checked at call time but not inside the dispatched
Render/Loaded callbacks. During StrataThink expand animations (MaxHeight
0→5000 over 300ms), the extent fluctuates rapidly. If the guard passed
at call time but the extent changed by execution time, the scroll would
fire with stale values and push the first message out of view.

Changes:
- Extract IsShortTranscript() helper for consistent re-checks
- Re-check the guard inside both dispatched callbacks with up-to-date
  layout values
- Increase ShortTranscriptMaxScroll from 300 to 600 to account for
  StrataThink animation expansion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix incorrect version check order in MVVMToolkitRemoveDuplicateAnalyzers target

MSBuild PropertyGroup elements are evaluated top-to-bottom, with later
assignments overwriting earlier ones. The >= 4.12 check was before the
>= 4.3 check, so for any Roslyn version >= 4.12 (including 5.0), the
4.12 result would be immediately overwritten by the 4.3 match. This
caused the wrong analyzer (roslyn4.3) to be selected instead of the
correct one (roslyn4.12).

Swap the order so the lower version (4.3) is checked first and the
higher version (4.12) is checked last, ensuring the highest compatible
analyzer wins.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enforce minimum code coverage threshold in CI pipeline (#7188)

* Enforce minimum code coverage threshold in CI pipeline

Add a coverage gate to the CodeCoverage_Upload stage that fails the build
when total statement coverage drops below a configured minimum.

- New Test-CodeCoverageThreshold.ps1 script parses 'go tool cover -func'
  output and fails if coverage is below the threshold
- New MinimumCoveragePercent parameter on code-coverage-upload.yml
  (default 0 = disabled)
- Set threshold to 80% in release-cli.yml to validate the mechanism
  (will be adjusted to the actual floor after verification)

Fixes #7187

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix coverage threshold step to use PowerShell task directly

The previous approach nested 'pwsh -File' inside a pwsh inline step,
which caused $CoverageFile to be treated as a literal string instead
of the script parameter. Switch to PowerShell@2 task with targetType:
filePath so arguments are passed correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix PowerShell variable interpolation in go tool cover argument

Double-quote the '-func=$CoverageFile' argument so PowerShell expands
the variable. Without quotes, PowerShell passes the literal string
'$CoverageFile' to the go command.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Set workingDirectory to cli/azd for go tool cover module resolution

go tool cover -func needs to resolve Go module paths from the coverage
file. Run the threshold check from cli/azd/ where go.mod lives, and
pass the absolute path to cover.out.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Set threshold to 48% to match current baseline
- Add ValidateRange(0,100) on MinimumCoveragePercent parameter
- Find total line by 'total:' prefix instead of assuming last line
- Match both integer and decimal percentages with invariant culture parsing
- Add condition: succeededOrFailed() to PublishCodeCoverageResults so
  coverage reports are still uploaded when the threshold check fails

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: Tablet image uses official Ubuntu cloud rootfs — no debootstrap, ~5 min build

Use theme CSS variables in GTOrderMatrix and GTUltraView

Replaced all hardcoded colors (#1a1a2e, #111122, #151528, #555, etc.)
with CSS variable reads (--color-tracker-row-even, --color-tracker-row-odd,

regardless of which theme is active.

commit.message:
[rush][rush-published-versions-json-plugin] Add plugin-native global commands; add published versions JSON plugin. (#5701)

* Add functions to better support commands provided by global commands.

* Introduce rush-published-versions-json-plugin.

* fixup! Introduce rush-published-versions-json-plugin.

* fixup! Introduce rush-published-versions-json-plugin.

* fixup! Introduce rush-published-versions-json-plugin.

* fixup! Introduce rush-published-versions-json-plugin.

* Add "globalPlugin" command kind for plugin-native global commands

Introduces a new "globalPlugin" commandKind for command-line.json that can only
be used in Rush plugin command-line.json files. Unlike "global" commands, it has
no shellCommand — the implementation must be provided entirely by the plugin via
the runGlobalCustomCommand hook with setHandled().

- Added globalPluginCommandKind constant to RushConstants
- Added globalPluginCommand to JSON schema (no shellCommand/autoinstallerName)
- Added IGlobalPluginCommandJson type; at runtime, normalized to IGlobalCommandConfig
  with shellCommand="" and isPluginOnly=true (similar to bulk→phased conversion)
- Rejected globalPlugin in repo command-line.json (loadFromFileOrDefault)
- Updated plugin command-line.json to use the new kind
- Fixed plugin .npmignore to include command-line.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changefile for globalPlugin command kind

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR feedback: rename isPluginOnly to providedByPlugin

Rename isPluginOnly → providedByPlugin for clarity per review feedback.
Also update the first changefile comment to reflect the globalPlugin design.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split changefiles: API additions vs globalPlugin command kind

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add per-channel column support to pattern editor

- FormatChannel.columns allows channels to override global formatColumns
- ChannelSnapshot.columnSpecs carries per-channel specs to GL/Canvas2D workers
- Both renderers use chData.columnSpecs ?? ui.columns for rendering
- Channel widths now computed per-channel based on their column definitions
- Cursor caret positioning uses data-driven columns in format mode
- Header labels render per-channel column names (L/R for tables, Val for orders)
- GT Ultra order channels show single hex value column
- GT Ultra table channels (WAVE/PULSE/FLTR/SPEED) show L/R hex columns
- Restored GTOrderMatrix panel above pattern editor
- Click handling and keyboard input use per-channel columns

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
